description_long: This object is a stylized, dark brown flower with a dense, rounded bloom composed of numerous overlapping petals. The petals are arranged in a tight, radial pattern, creating a voluminous, almost spherical flower head. The surface of the petals appears matte and slightly rough, with subtle textural details that suggest a natural, organic form. The flower is supported by a single, thick stem that tapers toward the base, giving it a sturdy appearance. No leaves are visible on or near the stem, and the coloration is uniformly dark brown throughout. The overall form is reminiscent of a dahlia or chrysanthemum, with a highly layered and symmetrical petal arrangement.
description: A dark brown, spherical flower with densely layered petals and a thick, tapered stem. The petals are tightly packed in a radial pattern, giving the bloom a voluminous, textured appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rounded, dense bloom with overlapping petals and a straight, thick stem.
description_view_1: This view highlights the tapering of the stem and the symmetrical, spherical arrangement of the petals.
description_view_2: The bloom appears slightly more elongated, with the petal layering and stem curvature more visible.
description_view_3: Back view emphasizes the uniformity of the petal arrangement and the solid, matte texture of the flower.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a dense, radial configuration with multiple overlapping and layered whorls, creating a spherical and symmetrical bloom typical of composite flowers.
